summary: Lewis Hamilton's arrival injects Ferrari with momentum as the Scuderia chase their first win since Barcelona, testing whether the seven-time champion can sustain his resurgence at a circuit that traditionally favors top-line speed. McLaren's consistency threatens to capitalize on any Ferrari stumble, with Norris and Piastri positioned to capitalize on mistakes in the tight Spa-Francorchamps battle. A Ferrari victory would reshape the constructors' championship landscape and validate Hamilton's ability to deliver immediate results under pressure, while a McLaren one-two would extend their title advantage and expose whether Hamilton's Spanish success was merely a breakthrough or the start of a genuine Ferrari revival.
